# BusinessFlow — Notion brain snapshot

> Durable backup/mirror of the Notion brief ([source](https://www.notion.so/a72c8f26a29a40fd9e23febebc73e078)).

> **Snapshot Notion brain** GitHub Action (scheduled + manual).

> 📚 Standing rulebook: [📚 Lessons & Playbook](https://app.notion.com/p/38f829ebf7be81c5a62ccb3bdb63c603) — read before acting, append new lessons.

**Last advanced: 2026-07-01** (auto-advance run).


## 🎯 End Goal

BusinessFlow Studio is a live, production SaaS website builder for small businesses (Angular 21 SSR + Firebase + Stripe, deployed on Google Cloud Run at [businessflow-722923667291.us-central1.run.app](http://businessflow-722923667291.us-central1.run.app/)), monetized via three subscription tiers — Free, Pro ($14/mo), Business ($22/mo) — gated on AI tools, enquiry/service limits, and admin extras. The end goal is to take it from "feature-complete and CI-green in a dev/test posture" to a fully launched, paying-customer product: production Stripe keys live, custom domain wired up, auto-deploy working end-to-end, and legal docs (ToS/Privacy/AUP) lawyer-reviewed and published.


## 🗺️ Roadmap (Now / Next / Later)

**Now (this week)**

- Merge PR #7 (disabled preview-button label fix) — clean, CI-green, merge-ready
- Configure GitHub Actions WIF secrets (`WIF_PROVIDER`, `WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` in GCP project `sitebuilder-b2ee6`) so `main` auto-deploys to Cloud Run instead of requiring manual `gcloud run deploy`
- Prune merged/stale `claude/whats-next-*` branches (keep `claude/whats-next-dq0gdx` untouched only if still flagged do-not-merge; otherwise merge or drop per latest Repo Watch note)
- Merge/land the rebased `claude/whats-next-dq0gdx` branch (CI gate + dev config + perf-batch `server.ts`) now that it's merge-ready
**Next (this month)**

- Switch Stripe from test keys to live production keys before accepting real payments
- Engage an NZ-qualified lawyer to review the ToS / Privacy Policy / Acceptable Use Policy drafts and fill in bracketed placeholders (entity name, NZBN, liability cap, etc.); prepare a Data Processing Addendum
- Choose and register a production domain name; confirm "BusinessFlow" has no trademark conflict (see Trademark Clearance page)
- Validate Free / Pro ($14) / Business ($22) pricing against comparables (monetization research)
- Wire enquiry email notifications end-to-end (webhook path built on `feat/enquiry-webhook-notifications`; needs an n8n webhook + `ENQUIRY_WEBHOOK_URL` env var set on Cloud Run)
**Later (3-6+ months)**

- Automate custom-domain mapping (domain verification + Cloud Run domain mapping) behind the Business tier, replacing the current text-field-only DNS instructions
- Expand AI content generation (services/FAQs shipped; hero/pricing/trust/cta recommendation types still copy-paste only — extend auto-insert to remaining types)
- Post-launch growth: iterate on the AI Growth Coach recommendations and SEO/crawler-meta work already shipped, based on real customer usage once live

## ✅ Session wrap — 2026-07-01 (Cowork auto-advance) — crawler/social meta shipped off main

**Single unblocked next step, self-contained off ****`main`**** (no stacking).** Picked the explicitly-flagged SEO follow-up: server-rendered `<title>` + Open Graph / Twitter Card meta for generated public sites, so link-preview bots stop unfurling every site as the generic "BusinessFlow Studio" shell.

- **What:** `/site/:uid` and `/site/:uid/pages/:slug` now detect crawler/unfurl user-agents (Googlebot, facebookexternalhit, Twitterbot, Slackbot, LinkedInBot, WhatsApp, Discord, Bing…) and serve them the app shell with the site's real title/description/canonical/`og:image` injected into `<head>`. **Real browsers are untouched** — they fall through to the normal Angular SSR path, so hydration is unaffected. Description falls back tagline → description → "name — type" → generic; `og:image` from the branding logo.
- **Design (matches the robots/sitemap pattern):** pure, unit-tested helpers in `src/server-meta.ts` (`isCrawler`, `resolveSiteMeta`, `renderMetaTags`, `injectMetaTags`) + `src/server-meta.spec.ts`; thin Express wiring in `server.ts` that reads+caches the built shell once. `CLAUDE.md` known-issues updated (SEO follow-up marked done).
- **Pushed:** branch **`claude/whats-next-crawler-meta-68296`** (commit `822f594`) — **confirmed on remote via ****`git ls-remote`** (local == remote SHA). The workforce-merge pipeline runs the CI gate (lint+test+build) on push to `claude/**` and lands it on `main` if green; `api.github.com` stays blocked for the sandbox so no PR was opened (by design).
- **Verification (honest labelling — lesson #4):** ✅ real code executed — 31/31 logic assertions pass by running the actual `server-meta.ts` via `node --experimental-strip-types`; ✅ syntax valid — `node --check --experimental-strip-types` on `server.ts` + both new files (rc 0); ✅ manual type review of the wiring. ⚠️ **Could NOT run ****`ng lint`**** / ****`ng test`**** / prod ****`ng build`**** in-sandbox** — the in-sandbox `npm install` is incomplete/broken (tsc, esbuild JS, and vitest entry all missing from a partial `node_modules`; same documented infra limit). The CI gate runs the full lint+test+build before the pipeline merges, so a red gate blocks the merge — `main` can't be silently broken.
- **Durable backup:** the code is durably stored as the pushed+confirmed remote branch (lesson #9 satisfied via branch push). Patch also written to Cowork outputs (`crawler-meta.patch`, ephemeral).
- **No stacking (lesson #5):** built on a fresh branch cut from current `origin/main` (`05e764f`); remote only had `main` + 2 stale branches — the custom-domain / enquiry / dq0gdx branches are already pruned from the remote, so nothing was stacked.

## ✅ Session wrap — 2026-06-30 (Claude) — Workflow hardening + growth auto-insert finished

**Workflow conventions added to the brain (****`CLAUDE.md`****) so every session/agent works the same way:**

- **One fresh branch per task — never reuse a branch.** Cut a new `claude/<task>-*` branch from current `origin/main` for each task. *Why:* the workforce auto-merge pipeline merges every push to `claude/**` into `main` and then **deletes the branch**. Reusing one branch fights this — it can merge a half-finished snapshot, delete the branch out from under an active session, and force rebase/force-push churn (all observed this session on the single pinned branch). A unique branch per task makes each push a complete unit the pipeline merges once and cleans up. **Push only when done and green** (lint + test + build) so the pipeline never grabs work-in-progress.
- **Web sessions now auto-install deps.** Added a **SessionStart hook** (`.claude/hooks/session-start.sh`, registered in `.claude/settings.json`) that runs `npm install` before each Claude Code on the web session — lint/test/build work immediately, no manual install. Web-only (gated on `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE`), idempotent, synchronous (can switch to async for faster startup). `settings.json` also allowlists routine read-only `git`/`npm` commands to cut permission prompts.
- **Gotcha documented:** run tests via `npm test` / `ng test`, **not** bare `vitest` — the Angular builder owns the vitest config; bare `vitest` finds no tests.
- ⚠️ **Open item for Aden:** the Cowork session/task config currently *pins* sessions to one fixed branch name (`claude/brain-dump-9snv5g`). The brain now prescribes branch-per-task, but making it actually happen needs that session config to stop hardcoding one branch.
**Growth-recommendation auto-insert — finished all viable types.** Building on the FAQ/Service work: `hero` drafts now auto-insert (headline → `tagline`, subtitle → `description`), `cta` → `ctaText` button label (draft reduced to a short phrase), `trust` → a 5-star testimonial. The draft-parsing logic was extracted into pure, unit-tested functions (`parseHeroDraft`, `parseCtaLabel`, `parseTestimonialDraft`). `pricing` recs have no structured insert target (free-form guidance), so they get a **"Review services"** deep-link to `/admin/content?tab=services` instead (Admin → Content now honours `?tab=`).

**Verified:** `npm run lint` ✅, `npm test` ✅ 68/68, prod `npm run build` ✅. Work pushed on branch `claude/brain-dump-9snv5g`; earlier commits already integrated into `main` via the hourly auto-merge.

- **Diagnosed + fixed the broken deploy pipeline.** The `Deploy to Cloud Run` workflow had been **failing on every push to ****`main`** (deploy job died at GCP auth — WIF secrets empty while `GCP_PROJECT_ID` was set, so the `if` gate let it run half-configured). Past sessions saw the passing *build* job and wrongly logged "deploys green."
- **Fix shipped:** PR **#15** merged to `main` (commit `c5052fa`) — re-gates the deploy job to **skip (stay green) unless ****`GCP_PROJECT_ID`**** + both ****`WIF_PROVIDER`**** & ****`WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT`**** are set**, auto-resuming once they are.
- **VERIFIED GREEN:** the post-merge run (#35) on `main` = `build-and-check` ✅ **success**, `deploy` ⏭️ **skipped** (not failed). The red-X on every push is gone.
**⚠️ Still true — production is NOT auto-updating.** Auto-deploy remains OFF until WIF is configured, so every merged PR (incl. #7's label) sits on `main` but is **not live**. To ship, Aden picks one:

1. **Manual deploy now:** `gcloud run deploy businessflow --source . --region us-central1 --set-env-vars="NG_ALLOWED_HOSTS=*"`
1. **Enable auto-deploy:** create the WIF provider + deployer SA in GCP project `sitebuilder-b2ee6`, add repo secrets `WIF_PROVIDER` + `WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` (guide in `deploy.yml` header) → pushes to `main` then deploy automatically.
**Housekeeping left for Aden:** prune the merged/stale `claude/whats-next-*` + `feat/ai-content-generation` + `claude/lucid-gauss-mj0y7a` + `claude/awesome-bell-t855cy` branches; do NOT merge `claude/whats-next-dq0gdx` or `fix/view-live-page-and-dashboard-setup` (stale/superseded).

## 🚨 Deploy pipeline is RED — corrected 2026-06-29 (Claude, verified via Actions API)

**The "Deploy to Cloud Run runs #21–#25 succeeded / CI green" notes in older entries are WRONG.** Verified against the GitHub Actions API: the `deploy.yml` workflow's **`build-and-check`**** job passes**, but the **`deploy`**** job FAILS on every push to ****`main`** (runs `84cd2e0`, `03ac1f2`, `6ea1ed3`, `b701f76`, `e2e2528` … all `conclusion: failure`). Earlier sessions saw the green build job and assumed the whole workflow was green.

- **Exact error:** `google-github-actions/auth failed with: the GitHub Action workflow must specify exactly one of "workload_identity_provider" or "credentials_json"` → the **WIF secrets are empty**.
- **Root cause:** repo **variable** `GCP_PROJECT_ID` is set (`sitebuilder-b2ee6`), but the **secrets** `WIF_PROVIDER` + `WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` were never added. The deploy job's gate (`if: vars.GCP_PROJECT_ID != ''`) checked only the variable, so it activated half-configured and hard-failed at auth.
- **Consequence:** **auto-deploy has NEVER worked.** Every merged PR (incl. #7's label, the webhook, business-profile editor, Sentry, etc.) is on `main` but **NOT live** until someone runs a manual `gcloud run deploy`. This matches `CLAUDE.md` Known-issues ("manual gcloud is the current method") — the auto-deploy notes were the inaccurate part.
**Fix prepared (Claude):** branch **`claude/notion-brain-prs-vcudr7`**, commit `5af0459` — re-gates the deploy job so it **skips (stays green) until GCP_PROJECT_ID + both WIF secrets are present**, and auto-resumes once they are. *PR not opened (awaiting Aden).*

**To actually ship to production, Aden picks ONE:**

1. **Wire up auto-deploy:** create the WIF pool/provider + deployer service account in GCP project `sitebuilder-b2ee6`, then add repo secrets `WIF_PROVIDER` + `WIF_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` (setup guide is in `deploy.yml`'s header). Then every push to `main` deploys.
1. **Manual deploy (works today):** `gcloud run deploy businessflow --source . --region us-central1 --set-env-vars="NG_ALLOWED_HOSTS=*"` — ships current `main` (incl. PR #7) to the live URL now.

## 🚀 Launch Prep checklist

- [ ] **Monetization research** — validate pricing/tiers (Free / Pro $14 / Business $22) against comparables
- [ ] **Name / trademark check** — confirm "BusinessFlow" is available + no trademark conflict
- [ ] **Domain name** — choose + register the production domain
- [ ] **Switch Stripe to production** — swap test keys for live keys before real payments
- [ ] **Remaining go-live steps** — configure GitHub Actions WIF secrets; complete final launch checklist
- [ ] **Fix broken view-live-page error** — 🟡 FIX PUSHED (awaiting manual PR merge). Branch `fix/view-live-page-and-dashboard-setup`. Root cause: the Page Builder "Preview Site" action was gated on a claimed URL slug with *no UID fallback*, so `previewUrl` was `null` and the button was disabled/dead whenever no slug had been claimed (incl. when onboarding's best-effort slug claim failed) — "doesn't navigate to the URL." Fix: builder `previewUrl` now falls back to `/site/{uid}` (the public route + `/api/site/:uid` already resolve a raw UID via `resolveUid`), gated on `isSetupComplete()` only; `site-view` now subscribes to the route paramMap (not a one-time snapshot) and catches load errors inline so navigating between sites reliably reloads. Lint + dev build + 41 unit tests pass. Same PR also fixes the dashboard-customisation issue (below). — see 🐛 Bugs
"

> BusinessFlow Studio — SaaS website builder for small businesses. Active project. The repo's own `CLAUDE.md` is the source of truth; this page is the quick brief.


# Facts

- **Repo:** kingithegreat/bussnessbuilder (app at repo root, no subfolder)
- **Live URL:** [https://businessflow-722923667291.us-central1.run.app](https://businessflow-722923667291.us-central1.run.app/)
- **Stack:** Angular 21 SSR (standalone components, signals, `@`-syntax) + Express server, Tailwind v4 + Angular Material 21, Firebase Auth + Firestore + Storage, Google Gemini AI, Stripe subscriptions. Deployed on Google Cloud Run.
- **Per-user data:** Firestore at `/users/{uid}/businessData/main` (+ sub-docs).

# Commands


```bash
npm install                 # needs .npmrc legacy-peer-deps (@angular/fire@20 vs Angular 21)
npm run dev                 # dev server, port 3000
npm run build               # production SSR build
npm run lint                # ESLint
npm test -- --watch=false   # Vitest
```


# Core rules

- ALL site-customization features (branding, page/form builder, sections, layouts, colours, fonts) are on EVERY tier. Only AI tools, enquiry/service limits, and admin extras are tier-gated.
- firebase-admin, Stripe, nodemailer, and @google/genai MUST stay lazy-loaded in `server.ts` (CommonJS breaks Angular's ESM build).
- Firestore DocumentData uses bracket notation (`data['field']`).

# Tiers

- **Free:** template-only AI, 3 services, 10 enquiries
- **Pro ($14/mo):** real AI, unlimited services/enquiries, export/import
- **Business ($22/mo):** priority AI, custom domain, analytics, remove branding

# 🔧 Session 2026-06-29 — view-live-page + dashboard customisation

- **Branch:** `fix/view-live-page-and-dashboard-setup` (pushed). ⚠️ **MANUAL:** Aden opens the PR to `main` → [open PR](https://github.com/kingithegreat/bussnessbuilder/pull/new/fix/view-live-page-and-dashboard-setup). Merging auto-deploys via `deploy.yml`.
- **Issue 1 (view-live-page):** Root cause was *not* the public route/SSR (verified `/site/:uid` SSR returns 200 + `resolveUid` accepts a raw UID, locally and on the live Cloud Run URL). The defect was the **entry point**: builder "Preview Site" `previewUrl` returned `null` without a claimed slug ("no UID fallback"), so the button was disabled/dead → "doesn't navigate to the URL." Now falls back to `/site/{uid}` and gates on `isSetupComplete()` only (matches the header "View live site" button). Also hardened `site-view.component` to react to route paramMap changes + catch load errors inline.
- **Issue 2 (customise anything from dashboard):** Business name, type, tagline, email, phone, service area, address, opening hours + description were only editable in the first-run setup wizard. Added a **"Business Profile" editor card** to dashboard **Settings** (saves via `DataService.updateProfile`) so they're editable any time.
- **Verify:** `npm run lint` ✅, `ng build --configuration development` ✅, `npm test` ✅ 41/41. (Full prod `ng build` only blocked locally by network-restricted Google Fonts inlining — CI will run it.)
- **Files:** `src/app/admin-builder.component.ts`, `src/app/site-view.component.ts`, `src/app/admin-settings.component.ts`.

# 🔧 Session 2026-06-29 (on-demand run) — enquiry webhook notifications

- **Branch:** `feat/enquiry-webhook-notifications` (committed locally in sandbox; **NOT pushed** — this environment has no GitHub credentials, so push is the manual step). Commit `7ab327a`.
- **What:** wired the long-pending "enquiry email notifications" next-step via an **n8n-style webhook** (no SMTP, no WIF). New `POST /api/site/:uid/enquiry` does a fire-and-forget POST to `ENQUIRY_WEBHOOK_URL` with `{event, uid, notificationEmail, enquiry:{...}}` when the owner has opted in. Root issue it solves: the existing SMTP path silently no-ops because `SMTP_*` env vars were never configured, so owners never got notified.
- **Design:** new pure helper `src/server-notify.ts` (`buildEnquiryWebhookRequest`) gated on existing `emailOnNewEnquiry` + `notificationEmail` prefs (no new UI); shares the single notifications-doc read with the SMTP path. Documented `ENQUIRY_WEBHOOK_URL` in `.env.example`.
- **Files:** `src/server-notify.ts`, `src/server-notify.spec.ts`, `src/server.ts`, `.env.example`.
- **Verification reached:** reproduce-first (stub → tests RED → implemented → GREEN); lint clean; **46/46 unit tests pass** (5 new, was 41); dev SSR build OK (bundles `server.ts`); **runtime confirmed** — local HTTP listener received the correct JSON POST and the opt-out path dispatched nothing. (Full prod `ng build` not run; CI will. SMTP path unchanged.)
- **⚠️ MANUAL (Aden):** apply patch + push, then open PR → [open PR](https://github.com/kingithegreat/bussnessbuilder/pull/new/feat/enquiry-webhook-notifications). Patch + apply instructions saved to the Cowork outputs folder (`enquiry-webhook-notifications.patch`, `APPLY-enquiry-webhook.md`). After merge: create an n8n webhook, set Cloud Run env `ENQUIRY_WEBHOOK_URL`, owners toggle the pref in Settings.

## ⚖️ Legal docs — DRAFTS exist, need lawyer review (added 2026-06-29)

Core legal-doc **drafts** generated for BusinessFlow: **Terms of Service**, **Privacy Policy**, and **Acceptable Use Policy** (markdown, in the Cowork outputs folder).

- ⚠️ **Status: UNREVIEWED DRAFTS — not legal advice. Must be reviewed by a NZ-qualified lawyer before publishing.**
- Tailored to the website-builder model: users responsible for their own sites/content; BusinessFlow acts as **processor/agent** for site-visitor data.
- ToS includes: account terms, acceptable use, payment/subscription + auto-renewal, **liability cap = fees paid in prior 12 months**, "as is" warranty disclaimer, user indemnification, termination, NZ governing law.
- Each doc has **[LAWYER]** flags and **[BRACKETED]** placeholders (legal entity name, NZBN, emails, addresses, cap amount) to fill in.
- Key NZ angles flagged for the lawyer: **Consumer Guarantees Act 1993** (B2B contract-out under s 43 must be in writing + fair & reasonable; consumer guarantees can't be excluded), **Fair Trading Act 1986** (s 13(i) offence to purport to contract out improperly), **Privacy Act 2020** (IPP 12 overseas disclosure for Stripe/hosting; notifiable breach regime; access/correction rights; note new IPP 3A effective 1 May 2026), and **GDPR** basics if serving EU/UK users (consider a separate DPA).
- **Next step:** engage a lawyer to review; prepare a Data Processing Addendum (DPA) for the processor relationship.
- 📄 ⚖️ Trademark Clearance + Name Shortlist — "Business Flow" (2026-06-29)
> Preliminary brand/trademark clearance for the BusinessFlow website-builder SaaS, plus a vetted shortlist of distinctive alternative names. Aden is NZ-based, serving global users. This is a knock-out search, **not** legal advice — confirm with a full clearance search + registrar check before committing.


## TL;DR / Verdict

**"Business Flow" is safe-ish to *****use***** but unwise to *****build a brand on*****.** No live exact "Business Flow" trademark was found in the US software classes (the only exact USPTO mark is dead/abandoned), so day-one legal risk is low. But the term is **descriptive and extremely crowded** — you cannot realistically own or defend it, multiple live businesses already use near-identical names, and you'll fight them forever in search results. **Recommendation: switch to a distinctive coined name now, before sinking marketing spend into the current one.** Top 3 alternatives: **Sitora, Pagora, Vossi.**


---


# PART 1 — Clearance read on "Business Flow"


## What's on the trademark registers

**USPTO (United States)**

- Exact word mark **"BUSINESS FLOW"** — Serial **75236557**, owner **Melita International Corporation**, filed 5 Feb 1997, Nice classes **009 / 037 / 042** (computer software for organizing, directing and controlling communication; related installation/dev services). **Status: DEAD / ABANDONED** (abandoned 16 Feb 2002).
- No other **live** exact "Business Flow" mark for website/SaaS software was found.
- The wider **"…FLOW"** field is enormous — Trademarkia returns ~30,000 hits for "business flow", with thousands of live marks in classes 9 (5,051), 35 (11,796) and 42 (5,956). Many live FLOW-formative marks exist (FLOW, SMART FLOW, J FLOW, FLOW YOUR BUSINESS, etc.) but none owns the words "Business Flow" for a website builder.
**IPONZ (New Zealand) & WIPO Global Brand Database**

- Both official registers are JavaScript-only apps that could not be machine-queried in this session. **Flagged for a manual check**: search "business flow" / "businessflow" in classes 9 & 42 at [**iponz.govt.nz**](http://iponz.govt.nz/)** → Trade Mark Check** and [**branddb.wipo.int**](http://branddb.wipo.int/).
- Given how descriptive the term is, it is unlikely anyone holds an *enforceable* exact word mark for website-building in NZ either — but verify before relying on it.

## The field is crowded (confirms & expands earlier research)

- [**mybusinessflow.com**](http://mybusinessflow.com/) — live US product (AI call-answering / front desk for home-service businesses).
- [**mybusinessflow.app**](http://mybusinessflow.app/) — cash-flow forecasting tool.
- [**business-flow.co.uk**](http://business-flow.co.uk/) — business management/control software.
- **Business Flow Limited** — UK company no. **16153188**, incorporated 27 Dec 2024; registered activity = business & domestic software development, IT consultancy, web portals. **Directly overlapping field.**
- Adjacent: **BizFlow** ([bizflow.com](http://bizflow.com/), low-code workflow), **SAP Business Workflow**, and many "…flow" SaaS names.

## Why descriptive marks are weak (plain English)

"Business Flow" is ordinary English describing what the software does (workflow for businesses). Trademark law ranks names from **generic → descriptive → suggestive → arbitrary → fanciful (coined)**. The further right, the stronger and easier to register. Descriptive names sit near the weak end: registries (USPTO, IPONZ) **refuse or restrict** them unless you prove **"acquired distinctiveness" / secondary meaning** — years of use plus evidence that the public links the words specifically to you. Even if you do get a registration, a descriptive mark is **weak to defend**: you can't stop others using the ordinary words, and courts give it a narrow scope.


## Verdict — is it safe to use, and what's the real risk?

**Safe to USE as a trading name:** Probably low legal risk *right now*, because (a) no live exact registration was found in the US software classes, and (b) the term is so generic that no one can easily claim exclusive rights to it.

**But you can't OWN or DEFEND it.** The realistic risks:

1. **Cease-and-desist / opposition exposure** — low-to-moderate. If you expand into the turf of an existing user (esp. UK *Business Flow Limited* or *mybusinessflow*), one of them could push back. Unlikely to win a monopoly over the words, but it can still cost you time, money, and a forced rebrand.
1. **No exclusivity** — you can't stop copycats or competitors using near-identical names. Your brand can be diluted at will.
1. **SEO / brand confusion** — you'll compete for search rankings against multiple "business flow" entities; customers may land on a competitor. Hard to build a clean, findable brand.
1. **Investor / exit red flag** — an unregistrable, crowded brand is a diligence weakness if you ever raise money or sell.
**Bottom line:** usable, but a poor long-term brand. Rebrand to something distinctive before you invest in marketing.


## If you keep it — how to register

- **Where:** **IPONZ** (your home base, ~NZ$70–100 per class, filed online) and **USPTO** (~US$350 per class, TEAS Plus) for the US market. For multiple countries, file a NZ base application then extend via the **Madrid Protocol** (WIPO).
- **Classes:** **9** (downloadable software) and **42** (SaaS / non-downloadable web software + software design) — the two core classes for a website builder. Add **35** if you also offer business/marketing services.
- **Timeline:** NZ ~6 months if unopposed; US ~8–18 months. Both include examination plus a public **opposition window**.
- **The hurdle:** expect a **descriptiveness objection** (s.18 NZ / §2(e)(1) US). You'd have to argue acquired distinctiveness or disclaim the words — costly and uncertain. A **stylized logo** is easier to register than the plain words, but it still won't give you rights over the words "business flow" themselves.

---


# PART 2 — Distinctive alternative names

**Why switch:** coined/suggestive names are far more protectable (registrable + defensible) and easier to rank for. **Method:** each name was checked for an existing software/web brand (a trademark + .com proxy) via web search, and several .coms were probed directly. Note: short coined .coms are mostly held by domainers — expect to use **.app / .io** or a modified .com (e.g. `getX`, `Xhq`, `tryX`), and run a final **registrar/WHOIS check** before committing.


## Shortlist


## Best 3 all-round picks

**Sitora · Pagora · Vossi** — all distinctive (so registrable and defensible), no clear conflicts found, brandable, and the most likely to have a usable domain (check `.com`; `.app`/`.io` are strong fallbacks).


## Names rejected during vetting (so the work is visible)

- **Already a website/site brand:** Plynth (an AI website builder), Sitesmith, Pagewright, Brixly, Webora, Sitelio.
- **Crowded software brands:** Lumora, Klyro, Sprynt, Orenda, Nuvora, Welo.
- **Too close to an existing mark:** Tildra (≈ *Tilda*, a major website builder), Brevio (exists; ≈ *Brevo*).

---


## Disclaimer

This is a preliminary knock-out search, not legal advice, and I'm not a lawyer. The official IPONZ and WIPO registers couldn't be machine-queried here and need a manual check. Before committing to any name, do (or have an IP attorney do) a full clearance search in NZ and your target markets, plus a registrar domain check.

- Business Flow Limited (UK Companies House 16153188): [https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/16153188](https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/16153188)

- 📄 🏷️ BusinessFlow Naming — Decision Brief
> Decide in 5 minutes. Synthesises the 2026-06-29 [Trademark Clearance + Name Shortlist](https://app.notion.com/p/38e829ebf7be81bdb61bdc8b7a20a253). Not legal advice.


## ⏱️ The call in one line

**Rebrand now, pre-launch, to ****`Sitora`****.** "Business Flow" is usable but unownable — and changing it is never cheaper than it is today, with zero users and zero SEO equity to lose.


---


## 1. The trade-off, sharp

**Keep "Business Flow" — the real cost:**

- **Can't trademark or defend it.** Descriptive ("workflow for businesses"); registries refuse descriptive marks without years of proven acquired distinctiveness, and even if granted the scope is narrow — you can't stop copycats.
- **Already crowded out** in your exact field: `mybusinessflow.com` (US), `business-flow.co.uk`, and **Business Flow Limited** (UK company #16153188, Dec 2024, web/software dev — direct overlap). Plus BizFlow, SAP Business Workflow, ~30k "…flow" marks.
- **Weak SEO forever** — you fight all of them for your own name; customers land on competitors.
- **Diligence red flag** if you ever raise or sell.
- Premium `.com` effectively unavailable, so you'd launch on a compromised domain anyway.
**Rebrand NOW (pre-launch):** a few hours of code/string/asset swaps (most automatable by Claude) + ~US$10–25 for a domain. No users, no SEO, no marketing equity to lose.

**Rebrand LATER (post-launch):** broken inbound links + dead SEO, confused users, redoing store listings / legal docs / marketing / portfolio, lost recognition, 301 debt. Cost rises every day you have traffic.

> **Timing:** the name touches the domain, repo, app strings, Stripe/Firebase labels, and the legal drafts — all cheapest to set right **before** the first user and first backlink exist. You're at that moment now. This is the cheapest this decision will ever be.


---


## 2. Recommendation


### ✅ Rebrand to **Sitora**

- **Why:** Suggestive of "site" without being descriptive → **registrable and defensible**, the opposite of "Business Flow." Soft, modern, easy to say/spell, brandable.
- **Verification (sniff-test, not legal advice):** Web search found **no software/SaaS or website-builder brand** on "Sitora" — clean in TM classes 9 & 42. (Exists as a given name meaning "star" — harmless, not a competing product.) Automated `.com` lookup was inconclusive in-sandbox but the name resolves to **no live site** — do a 30-second registrar check at purchase; `sitora.app` / `sitora.io` are strong fallbacks.

### 🥈 Runner-up: **Pagora**

Evokes "page," memorable, TM-clean in software. Caveat: builders **Pagio** (UK) and **Pagy** exist in the same field — different spelling/sound but slightly more confusion risk than Sitora. 

*(Most-distinctive coined alternative: ****Vossi**** — but **`vossi.com`** looks registered, so **`.app`**/**`.io`**.)*


---


## 3. Migration checklist (if you rebrand)

Top-down order. Most code/content items Claude can execute once the name is confirmed.

**Trivial:** 1, 4, 7, 8. **Real work but mostly automatable:** 2, 3, 5, 9. **Aden-only (account clicks):** 1, 6, 7/8, repo rename. 

Rough total: **a couple of focused hours**, vs. days-to-weeks post-launch.


---


## 4. If you're not sure — safe default

Solo builder, pre-launch. Asymmetry is stark: rebrand now = ~2 hrs + ~$15; keep an unownable name = lost defensibility, weak SEO, and a far more painful rename later.

**Default: register ****`sitora.com`**** (or ****`sitora.app`****) today and let Claude run the find-and-replace across app, legal docs, and repo.** If you freeze on the name, **Sitora is the safe pick.** Don't ship "Business Flow."


---

**Check ****`sitora.com`**** at your registrar and buy it (or ****`sitora.app`****).** Then tell Claude the name + domain → one PR swaps every "BusinessFlow" reference across the app, legal drafts, and repo.
